research.description: Research the topic '{topic}'. Your deliverable is a comprehensive research notes file at workspace/research_notes.md. Structure your notes under these sections: (1) Overview and Key Definitions, (2) Major Frameworks and Their Architecture, (3) Comparative Analysis and Trade-offs, (4) Production Patterns and Challenges, (5) Bibliography. Cite at least 8 sources using [AuthorYear] inline citation format. Minimum 1500 words total.
research.expected_output: A structured Markdown file at workspace/research_notes.md with 5 sections, minimum 1500 words, and at least 8 [AuthorYear] inline citations with full references in the Bibliography section.

write.description: Using the research notes at workspace/research_notes.md, write 6 Markdown chapters for an article on '{topic}'. Save each chapter to workspace/chapters/. Chapters: ch01_introduction.md (~400 words, introduce the topic), ch02_architectures.md (~500 words, agent architectures and topologies), ch03_frameworks.md (~500 words, LangChain vs CrewAI vs LangGraph comparison with a Markdown table), ch04_production.md (~400 words, production patterns including a mathematical relationship you mark with [NEEDS_FORMULA]), ch05_bidi.md (~400 words, write at least 3 full paragraphs in Hebrew mixed with English technical terms), ch06_casestudy.md (~300 words, case study of this pipeline). Each chapter must include at least 2 inline citations.
write.expected_output: 6 Markdown files in workspace/chapters/: ch01_introduction.md, ch02_architectures.md, ch03_frameworks.md (with comparison table), ch04_production.md (with [NEEDS_FORMULA] marker), ch05_bidi.md (with Hebrew paragraphs), ch06_casestudy.md.

edit.description: Review and polish all 6 chapters in workspace/chapters/. For each chapter: (1) enforce consistent terminology, (2) verify all [AuthorYear] citations are defined in the research notes bibliography, (3) ensure tables use pipe | syntax for clean LaTeX conversion, (4) verify ch05_bidi has at least 3 Hebrew paragraphs, (5) mark any mathematical relationship not yet marked as [NEEDS_FORMULA]. Save each polished chapter as ch0N_edited.md in workspace/chapters/.
edit.expected_output: 6 edited Markdown files in workspace/chapters/: ch01_edited.md through ch06_edited.md. Each improved for clarity, citation correctness, and LaTeX readiness.

latex_ch01.description:
GLOBAL OUTPUT RULES — read every rule before writing a single character:
R1. Your ENTIRE response is valid LaTeX. First character must be '%' or '\'. NO prose, preamble, or explanation before or after.
R2. FORBIDDEN: \verb|..| anywhere. Use \texttt{\textbackslash cmd\{arg\}} for code in text or tables.
R3. FORBIDDEN: bare 'l', 'r', 'c' column types in any tabular/longtable. Use ONLY p{Xcm}.
R4. FORBIDDEN: '|' column separators. Use booktabs: \toprule, \midrule, \bottomrule.
R5. Every longtable MUST have both \endfirsthead AND \endhead with the same header rows.
R6. Add \\[5pt] after every data row in tables for visual breathing room.
R7. Citations: use keys EXACTLY from: Russell2020, Wooldridge2009, WooldridgeJennings1995, WeiEtAl2022, Yao2023, Shinn2023, Park2023, Wang2023, Turner1986, CrewAI2024, LangChain2023, LangChain2024a, LangGraph2024, AutoGen2023, Anthropic2024b, Microsoft2025, Segal2026, Unicode2023, Polyglossia2023, AMS2023.
R8. FORBIDDEN undefined commands: NEVER invent \textplus, \textminus, or any \name not in standard LaTeX2e. Use literal + - characters.

Write chapter 1. Start with: \chapter{Introduction to Multi-Agent Orchestration}\label{ch:intro}
Include 3-4 \section{} headings. Minimum 400 words. Minimum 3 \cite{} citations.
Content: what multi-agent orchestration is, why single-agent systems are insufficient, key roles (researcher, writer, editor, LaTeX producer), and article roadmap.
Context from previous task will be appended below if available.
latex_ch01.expected_output: Valid LaTeX chapter 1 starting with \chapter{Introduction to Multi-Agent Orchestration}, no markdown fences, no undefined commands.

latex_ch02.description:
GLOBAL OUTPUT RULES — read every rule before writing a single character:
R1. Your ENTIRE response is valid LaTeX. First character must be '%' or '\'. NO prose before or after.
R2. FORBIDDEN: \verb|..| anywhere. Use \texttt{\textbackslash cmd} for inline code.
R3. FORBIDDEN: bare 'l', 'r', 'c' column types. Use ONLY p{Xcm}.
R4. FORBIDDEN: '|' column separators. Use \toprule, \midrule, \bottomrule.
R5. Every longtable MUST have \endfirsthead AND \endhead with identical header rows.
R6. Add \\[5pt] after every data row in tables.
R7. Citations: keys from: Russell2020, Wooldridge2009, WooldridgeJennings1995, WeiEtAl2022, Yao2023, Shinn2023, Park2023, Wang2023, Turner1986, CrewAI2024, LangChain2023, LangChain2024a, LangGraph2024, AutoGen2023, Anthropic2024b, Microsoft2025, Segal2026, Unicode2023, Polyglossia2023, AMS2023.
R8. FORBIDDEN: \textplus, \textminus, or any invented \command. Use literal + - characters.
R9. Table column widths must sum to ≤14cm total. For the 5-column topology table use EXACTLY: @{}p{2.8cm}p{2.8cm}p{2.6cm}p{2.0cm}p{3.9cm}@{}
R10. Keep math in table cells SHORT — max one term per cell. Example Latency cell: '$\sum_i \ell_i$' not a long O() expression.

Write chapter 2. Start with: \chapter{Agent Architectures and Topologies}\label{ch:architectures}
Include sections on: Linear Sequential, Hierarchical Manager-Worker, Peer-to-Peer Consensus, Tool-Augmented Specialists, Topology Comparison.
After the first paragraph of the first section include:
\begin{figure}[H]\centering\resizebox{\textwidth}{!}{\input{figures/crew_architecture.tikz}}\caption{CrewAI multi-agent architecture.}\label{fig:crew_architecture}\end{figure}
Include one \begin{equation}L_{\mathrm{seq}}=\sum_{i=1}^{N}\ell_i\label{eq:pipeline_latency}\end{equation}
Include a 5-column longtable comparing topologies (Topology, Latency, Parallelism, Complexity, Ideal For) with column spec @{}p{2.8cm}p{2.8cm}p{2.6cm}p{2.0cm}p{3.9cm}@{} and \\[5pt] after each row.
Minimum 4 \cite{} citations.
Context from previous task will be appended below if available.
latex_ch02.expected_output: Valid LaTeX chapter 2 with TikZ figure, latency equation, and 5-column longtable using @{}p{2.8cm}p{2.8cm}p{2.6cm}p{2.0cm}p{3.9cm}@{} with \\[5pt] row spacing.

latex_ch03.description:
GLOBAL OUTPUT RULES — read every rule before writing a single character:
R1. Your ENTIRE response is valid LaTeX. First character must be '%' or '\'. NO prose before or after.
R2. FORBIDDEN: \verb|..| anywhere. Use \texttt{\textbackslash cmd} for inline code.
R3. FORBIDDEN: bare 'l', 'r', 'c' column types. Use ONLY p{Xcm}.
R4. FORBIDDEN: '|' column separators. Use \toprule, \midrule, \bottomrule.
R5. Every longtable MUST have \endfirsthead AND \endhead with identical header rows.
R6. Add \\[8pt] after every data row in tables for readability.
R7. Citations: keys from: LangChain2023, LangChain2024a, LangGraph2024, CrewAI2024, AutoGen2023, Microsoft2025, Segal2026, Yao2023, Wang2023.
R8. FORBIDDEN: \textplus, \textminus, or any invented command.
R9. The 4-column framework table column spec MUST be EXACTLY: @{}p{2.5cm}p{3.5cm}p{3.8cm}p{4.0cm}@{} (total 13.8cm, fits A4).

Write chapter 3. Start with: \chapter{Orchestration Frameworks}\label{ch:frameworks}
Include sections on: LangChain and LCEL, CrewAI Role-Based Agents, LangGraph Stateful Graphs, Microsoft AutoGen, Framework Comparison.
The comparison section MUST include a longtable with spec @{}p{2.5cm}p{3.5cm}p{3.8cm}p{4.0cm}@{} comparing LangChain, CrewAI, LangGraph, AutoGen across columns: Framework, Architecture, Strengths, Best For. Add \\[8pt] after each framework row.
Minimum 400 words. Minimum 4 \cite{} citations.
Context from previous task will be appended below if available.
latex_ch03.expected_output: Valid LaTeX chapter 3 with longtable @{}p{2.5cm}p{3.5cm}p{3.8cm}p{4.0cm}@{} with \endfirsthead, \endhead, and \\[8pt] between rows.

latex_ch04.description:
GLOBAL OUTPUT RULES — read every rule before writing a single character:
R1. Your ENTIRE response is valid LaTeX. First character must be '%' or '\'. NO prose before or after.
R2. FORBIDDEN: \verb|..| anywhere.
R3. FORBIDDEN: bare 'l', 'r', 'c' column types. Use ONLY p{Xcm}.
R4. FORBIDDEN: '|' column separators. Use \toprule, \midrule, \bottomrule.
R5. Every longtable MUST have \endfirsthead AND \endhead.
R6. Add \\[5pt] after every data row in tables.
R7. Citations: keys from: Turner1986, Segal2026, CrewAI2024, LangGraph2024, Anthropic2024b, Wang2023, Yao2023, Russell2020.
R8. CRITICAL: EVERY mathematical expression MUST use \begin{equation}...\end{equation}. NEVER write math as plain text.
R9. FORBIDDEN: \textplus, \textminus, or any invented command. Use plain + or - in text; $+$ $-$ in math.

Write chapter 4. Start with: \chapter{Production Patterns and Challenges}\label{ch:production}
Include sections: Rate Limiting and Token Budgets, Gatekeeper Pattern, Retry and Circuit Breaker, Observability and Logging, Cost Optimization.
Include AT LEAST 3 \begin{equation} blocks for: token budget formula, cost model, retry probability.
Example: \begin{equation}C = \sum_{k=1}^{K}\bigl(c_{\mathrm{in}}\,t_{k}^{\mathrm{in}} + c_{\mathrm{out}}\,t_{k}^{\mathrm{out}}\bigr)\label{eq:cost}\end{equation}
Minimum 400 words. Minimum 3 \cite{} citations.
Context from previous task will be appended below if available.
latex_ch04.expected_output: Valid LaTeX chapter 4 with at least 3 \begin{equation} blocks, no plain-text math, no undefined commands.

latex_ch05.description:
GLOBAL OUTPUT RULES — read every rule before writing a single character:
R1. Your ENTIRE response is valid LaTeX. First character must be '%' or '\'. NO prose before or after.
R2. FORBIDDEN: \verb|..| anywhere — CRITICAL for this chapter. Use \texttt{\textbackslash cmd\{arg\}} for any LaTeX command shown as text.
R3. FORBIDDEN: bare 'l', 'r', 'c' column types. Use ONLY p{Xcm}.
R4. FORBIDDEN: '|' column separators. Use \toprule, \midrule, \bottomrule.
R5. Every longtable or tabular table MUST have \endfirsthead AND \endhead. Add \\[4pt] after each data row.
R6. Citations: keys from: Unicode2023, Polyglossia2023, Segal2026, CrewAI2024, Anthropic2024b.
R7. FORBIDDEN: \textplus, \textminus, or any invented command.
R8. BiDi rule A — English inside \texthebrew{}: EVERY English word inside \texthebrew{...} MUST be \foreignlanguage{english}{Word}. Example CORRECT: \texthebrew{פתרון \foreignlanguage{english}{LuaLaTeX} ו-\foreignlanguage{english}{Polyglossia}}. Example WRONG: \texthebrew{פתרון LuaLaTeX ו-Polyglossia} — this reverses the English characters.
R9. BiDi rule B — citations inside \begin{hebrew}: ALL \cite{} inside a \begin{hebrew}...\end{hebrew} block MUST be wrapped as \mbox{\cite{key}} — otherwise multi-digit numbers like [10] render as [01].
R10. Table in section 5.4 must use @{}p{3.8cm}p{5.8cm}p{4.0cm}@{} (total 13.6cm). Use \texttt{\textbackslash begin\{hebrew\}\ldots\textbackslash end\{hebrew\}} NOT \verb in table cells.

Write chapter 5 — the Hebrew BiDi chapter. EXACT structure required:

\addtocontents{toc}{%
  \protect\etocsetstyle{chapter}{}{}{\protect\addvspace{1.0em plus 1pt}%
   \begingroup\parindent0pt\noindent\bfseries%
   \etocpage\hfill{\color{uohblue}\etocname\hspace{1em}\etocnumber}%
  \par\endgroup}{}%
  \protect\etocsetstyle{section}{}{}{\begingroup\parindent0pt\noindent\normalfont\normalsize%
     \etocpage\hspace{0.5em}\protect\hebrewdotleaders\hspace{0.5em}%
     \etocname\hspace{0.5em}\etocnumber%
  \par\endgroup}{}%
}
\hebrewheadingformat
\chapter{\texthebrew{דו-כיוניות: עברית ואנגלית במערכות סוכנים}}
\label{ch:bidi}

Hebrew sections: \section[\texthebrew{כותרת}]{\texthebrew{כותרת}\mbox{\setLTR\enspace\thesection}}
Section 5.2 MUST be: \section[\texthebrew{פתרון \foreignlanguage{english}{LuaLaTeX} ו-\foreignlanguage{english}{Polyglossia}}]{\texthebrew{פתרון \foreignlanguage{english}{LuaLaTeX} ו-\foreignlanguage{english}{Polyglossia}}\mbox{\setLTR\enspace\thesection}}
Hebrew body: \begin{hebrew}...\end{hebrew}
Citations in Hebrew body: \mbox{\cite{key}} always.
After Hebrew sections add: \addtocontents{toc}{\protect\etocstandardlines}\defaultheadingformat
Section 5.4 uses: \section{Mixed-Language Example}

Content: 3 Hebrew sections (BiDi meaning, LuaLaTeX/Polyglossia solution, production challenges) + English section 5.4 with a table using @{}p{3.8cm}p{5.8cm}p{4.0cm}@{} and \texttt{} for command names. Minimum 3 \mbox{\cite{}} citations inside Hebrew blocks.
Context from previous task will be appended below if available.
latex_ch05.expected_output: Valid LaTeX chapter 5 with \mbox{\cite{}} in all Hebrew blocks, \foreignlanguage{english}{} for English in \texthebrew{}, \texttt{} instead of \verb, and table with @{}p{3.8cm}p{5.8cm}p{4.0cm}@{}.

latex_ch06.description:
GLOBAL OUTPUT RULES — read every rule before writing a single character:
R1. Your ENTIRE response is valid LaTeX. First character must be '%' or '\'. NO prose before or after.
R2. FORBIDDEN: \verb|..| anywhere.
R3. FORBIDDEN: bare 'l', 'r', 'c' column types. Use ONLY p{Xcm}.
R4. FORBIDDEN: '|' column separators. Use \toprule, \midrule, \bottomrule.
R5. Every longtable MUST have \endfirsthead AND \endhead. Add \\[4pt] after every data row.
R6. Citations: keys from: CrewAI2024, LangChain2023, LangGraph2024, Segal2026, Microsoft2025, Anthropic2024b, Wang2023.
R7. FORBIDDEN: \textplus, \textminus, or any invented command. Use plain + or - in cells.
R8. Metrics longtable MUST use @{}p{4cm}p{3cm}p{6.5cm}@{} with \\[4pt] after every row.

Write chapter 6. Start with: \chapter{Case Study: This Article's Production Pipeline}\label{ch:casestudy}
Include sections: Overview, The Crew (4 agents: Researcher, Writer, Editor, LaTeXAgent), Workflow Architecture, Key Engineering Decisions, Pipeline Metrics.
After the Workflow Architecture section include EXACTLY:
\begin{figure}[H]\centering\includegraphics[width=0.9\textwidth]{figures/pipeline_latency.png}\caption{Pipeline latency comparison: sequential vs fast (parallel Haiku) approach. Generated by Python/matplotlib.}\label{fig:pipeline_latency}\end{figure}
Include a longtable @{}p{4cm}p{3cm}p{6.5cm}@{} with caption, \toprule/\midrule/\bottomrule, \endfirsthead, \endhead, and \\[4pt] after every row. Rows: Pipeline Agents (4), Tasks Defined (11), Chapters Generated (6), LaTeX Passes (4), Target PDF Length (20 pages), Elapsed Time Fast (<10 min), Python Files (<=16), Test Coverage (>=85%). In cells use plain + not \textplus.
Minimum 300 words. Minimum 3 \cite{} citations.
Context from previous task will be appended below if available.
latex_ch06.expected_output: Valid LaTeX chapter 6 with pipeline_latency.png figure and longtable @{}p{4cm}p{3cm}p{6.5cm}@{} with \\[4pt] row spacing.

latex_bib.description:
OUTPUT RULES (follow exactly — violations cause compilation failure):
1. Your ENTIRE response must be a valid .bib file. First character must be '@'. NO prose before or after.
2. Generate @article, @book, or @misc entries for EVERY key from this set: Russell2020, Wooldridge2009, WooldridgeJennings1995, WeiEtAl2022, Yao2023, Shinn2023, Park2023, Wang2023, Turner1986, CrewAI2024, LangChain2023, LangChain2024a, LangGraph2024, AutoGen2023, Anthropic2024b, Microsoft2025, Segal2026, Unicode2023, Polyglossia2023, AMS2023.
3. No duplicate keys. No markdown fences. No trailing text after the last '}'.

Generate a complete BibTeX file with entries for ALL 20 citation keys listed above. Each entry needs: author, title, year, and at least one of: journal/booktitle/howpublished/url.
latex_bib.expected_output: Valid BibTeX content with @article/@book/@misc entries for all 20 citation keys.
